The Bygone Effigy starts asleep and follows this sequence:
Sleep
— does nothing on its first turn.
Wake
— wakes up and gains 10
Strength
.
Slashes
every turn after.
Strategy
Bygone Effigy
is a very dangerous Elite with a very simple weakness: hit it as hard as you can. You have two turns to attack it before it does anything; while it does gain 10 Strength on its second turn, this is just for flavor, as its 15(17) damage attack is only used after gaining Strength, making it functionally the same as a 25(27) attack. Your goal should be to deal as much damage as possible in those first three turns, in order to hopefully finish it off before it can attack.
Cards which do not deal damage are almost useless here, and your starter deck is generally not strong enough to win this battle on its own. Defend will only block 20% of the incoming damage from each attack from turn 3 onwards, and does nothing but increase
Slow
on the first two turns. Other defensive cards like
Dodge and Roll
or
Shrug It Off
are hardly any better, and adding them to your deck means you will draw attacks less often. If you plan to take on Elites in the Overgrowth, then your first priority should be to take as many strong attack cards as you can in order to be prepared in case you run into the Bygone Effigy. If you have not seen any strong attacks in early card rewards, it may be best to path away from elites until the second half of the Act rather than risk dying early.
If you cannot finish off the Bygone Effigy within three turns, then just keep hitting it as hard as you can and tank the damage. Don't play defensive cards unless you are absolutely certain you can finish it off on the next turn. Blocking 5 damage but then taking 25 damage because the Effigy survived an extra turn isn't worth it.
The
Slow
effect is usually not particularly impactful, but it does mean that extra card plays, such as from
Shiv
s, will slightly increase the damage the Bygone Effigy takes. It is rare that you will have enough of a combo deck put together this early in the game to play large numbers of cards each turn.

Infect

When Phrog Parasite dies,
Infested
triggers and 4
Wriggler
s are summoned. They will be stunned and do nothing on their first turn. The fight continues until all Wrigglers are defeated.
Notes
Whether the Wrigglers remain stunned on the player's next turn depends on whether the Phrog dies on the player's turn or its own turn.
If the Phrog is killed normally with an attack card, the Wrigglers will
not
be stunned on the player's next turn.
If the Phrog is killed by damage from an Orb, then the Wrigglers will
not
be stunned on the player's next turn.
If the Phrog dies to either
Poison
or
Doom
, then this counts as dying on its own turn, and so the Wrigglers
will
be stunned on the player's next turn.
Useful Cards
Frontloaded damage cards like
Perfected Strike
,
Backstab
, and
Defile
can be useful for taking down the Phrog Parasite before it can add too many copies of Infection to your deck, as well as for finishing off individual Wrigglers quickly.
Deathbringer
,
Deadly Poison
and other cards which apply
Poison
or
Doom
can give you an extra turn before the Wrigglers attack if the Phrog dies to their damage.
Attacks which target all enemies like
Dagger Spray
,
Bone Shards
or
Whirlwind
can damage all of the Wrigglers at once.
Cards which can exhaust or discard the Infections, such as
Burning Pact
or
Hidden Daggers
, can prevent them from damaging you.
Trivia
The
Paper Phrog
resembles the Phrog Parasite.
